name: Machine Economic Life Calculator
description: Determines the optimal economic life of a machine by balancing capital costs, operating costs, and productivity losses, aligned with ISO 15663, Lean, and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Purchase Price
inputs.businessContext: Initial capital investment for the machine, including installation and commissioning.

businessContext_i18n.en: Initial capital investment for the machine, including installation and commissioning.

inputs.label: Salvage Value at End of Life
inputs.businessContext: Estimated resale or scrap value at the end of the machine's useful life.

label_i18n.en: Salvage Value at End of Life

businessContext_i18n.en: Estimated resale or scrap value at the end of the machine's useful life.

inputs.label: Estimated Useful Life
inputs.businessContext: Expected number of years the machine will be operational before major overhaul or replacement.

businessContext_i18n.en: Expected number of years the machine will be operational before major overhaul or replacement.

inputs.label: Annual Operating Cost (Year 1)
inputs.businessContext: Includes energy, consumables, labor, and routine maintenance in the first year.

businessContext_i18n.en: Includes energy, consumables, labor, and routine maintenance in the first year.

inputs.label: Operating Cost Escalation Rate
inputs.businessContext: Annual increase in operating costs due to aging, inflation, and wear.

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual increase in operating costs due to aging, inflation, and wear.

inputs.label: Discount Rate (WACC)
inputs.businessContext: Weighted average cost of capital used for net present value calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Weighted average cost of capital used for net present value calculations.

inputs.label: Annual Production Units
inputs.businessContext: Number of units produced per year at full capacity.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of units produced per year at full capacity.

inputs.label: Revenue per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Selling price per unit produced by the machine.

businessContext_i18n.en: Selling price per unit produced by the machine.

inputs.label: Initial Defect Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of defective units in the first year, used to model quality degradation over time.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of defective units in the first year, used to model quality degradation over time.

inputs.label: Defect Rate Annual Increase
inputs.businessContext: Yearly increase in defect rate due to machine wear, impacting rework and scrap costs.

businessContext_i18n.en: Yearly increase in defect rate due to machine wear, impacting rework and scrap costs.

inputs.label: Maintenance Strategy
inputs.businessContext: Type of maintenance program in place, affecting downtime and cost patterns.

businessContext_i18n.en: Type of maintenance program in place, affecting downtime and cost patterns.

inputs.label: Utilization Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of available time the machine is actually producing.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of available time the machine is actually producing.

inputs.label: Energy Cost per kWh
inputs.businessContext: Cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour, used for energy consumption modeling.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour, used for energy consumption modeling.

inputs.label: Power Consumption
inputs.businessContext: Rated power consumption of the machine during operation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Rated power consumption of the machine during operation.

rules.message: Salvage value must be less than purchase price.
Useful life must be greater than zero.
Projected defect rate exceeds 100% within useful life.
Utilization rate must be between 0 and 100.

thresholds.message: Annual operating cost exceeds 30% of purchase price; consider replacement.
Utilization rate below 60%; investigate root causes.
Initial defect rate above 5%; Six Sigma DMAIC recommended.

formulas.name: Annual Operating Cost for Year n
formulas.description: Calculates operating cost for a given year n, accounting for escalation.

formulas.name: Annual Defect Cost
formulas.description: Cost of defects including rework, scrap, and lost revenue.

formulas.name: Annual Energy Cost
formulas.description: Energy cost based on power consumption, utilization, and hours.

formulas.name: Total Annual Cost for Year n
formulas.description: Sum of operating, defect, and energy costs for year n.

formulas.name: Net Present Value of Total Costs
formulas.description: Discounted sum of all annual costs over the useful life.

formulas.name: Optimal Economic Life
formulas.description: Year where the equivalent annual cost (EAC) is minimized, considering capital recovery.

formulas.name: Machine Economic Life (Years)
formulas.description: The year at which the machine should be replaced to minimize total lifecycle cost.

primary.label: Optimal Economic Life
primary.description: The recommended replacement year based on cost minimization.

capital_recovery_cost.label: Capital Recovery Cost
capital_recovery_cost.description: Annualized cost of initial investment minus salvage.

average_annual_operating_cost.label: Average Annual Operating Cost
average_annual_operating_cost.description: Mean operating cost over the economic life.

average_annual_defect_cost.label: Average Annual Defect Cost
average_annual_defect_cost.description: Mean defect-related cost over the economic life.

average_annual_energy_cost.label: Average Annual Energy Cost
average_annual_energy_cost.description: Mean energy cost over the economic life.

total_equivalent_annual_cost.label: Total Equivalent Annual Cost
total_equivalent_annual_cost.description: Sum of all annualized costs at the optimal life.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Quality Degradation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Increasing defect rate over time leads to hidden rework and scrap costs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Energy Inefficiency
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Aging machines often consume more energy per unit produced.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Maintenance Cost Escalation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Reactive maintenance strategies cause exponential cost increases.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Opportunity Cost of Downtime
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Unplanned downtime reduces effective utilization and revenue.

suggestedActions.description: Implement predictive maintenance using IoT sensors to reduce unplanned downtime.
Conduct a Six Sigma DMAIC project to reduce defect rate by 50%.
Evaluate energy-efficient upgrades or replacement if energy cost exceeds 20% of total cost.
Perform a sensitivity analysis on discount rate and escalation rate to validate economic life.
Benchmark machine OEE against industry standards (WERC/ISO 22400).

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Adjusted confidence based on input variability and data quality (0-100%).
